admin.access: Full CRUD on all entities, user management, billing

operator.access: Filtered by assigned accounts, no user management

manager.access: Read + limited write, scoped to their account

setup.title: 1 - Account Setup
setup.step1: Go to Settings → Integrations. Enter your Twilio Account SID, Auth Token, and the From Number (or Messaging Service SID) for this account.
setup.step2: Choose your number type: Local numbers are cheapest but have the lowest throughput. Toll-free numbers are recommended for most accounts. Short codes offer the highest volume but require a separate application.
setup.step3: If using a US 10-digit local number for A2P (application-to-person) messaging, you must complete A2P 10DLC registration with your carrier through Twilio's console. Without registration, messages may be filtered or blocked.
setup.step4: Set your business hours (start/end time) and timezone. The engine will never send messages outside these hours.
setup.step5: Set a Daily Send Limit (default 500). This is the max messages the account can send per day across all campaigns.
setup.step6: Copy the API Key (webhook_secret) from Settings. Paste this into your CRM integrations to authorize inbound leads.
setup.tip: The API key is shown once on generation. If lost, regenerate it — the old key will stop working immediately.

campaign.title: 2 - Creating a Campaign
campaign.step1: Go to Campaigns → New Campaign. Give it a name and select the channel (SMS or WhatsApp).

smsVsWhatsapp.title: SMS vs WhatsApp — when to use which
smsVsWhatsapp.sms: Higher open rates in the US/Canada, works with any phone, no template approval needed. Best for short, direct re-engagement.
smsVsWhatsapp.whatsapp: Preferred internationally, supports rich media, requires pre-approved message templates for the first outbound message. Best for longer conversational flows.

campaign.step2: Write your First Message template. Use merge tags: {{first_name}}, {{agent_name}}, {{service}}.
campaign.step3: For WhatsApp campaigns, your first message template must be approved by Meta before it can be sent. Submit it via the Twilio console under Messaging → Content Templates. Approval takes 1–3 business days.
campaign.step4: Add up to 3 Follow-up bumps. Set the delay in hours for each (e.g., 24h, 48h, 72h). Bumps are sent only if the lead hasn't replied.
campaign.step5: Set Active Hours, Daily Lead Limit, and optionally link a Prompt Library entry to customize the AI's persona.
campaign.step6: Set campaign status to Active when you're ready to start sending.
campaign.tip: Keep the first message short and personal. Avoid links in the first message — many carrier filters block them.

lifecycle.title: 3 - Lead Lifecycle & Statuses
lifecycle.pipelineDescription: Every lead sits in one of these pipeline stages visible on the Kanban board:

stages.new: Lead just arrived, not yet contacted.
stages.contacted: First message sent. Waiting for a reply.
stages.qualified: Lead shows intent — being guided to book.
stages.lost: Lead went cold or was not a fit.

lifecycle.automationDescription: Behind the scenes, leads also have an automation status that controls messaging:
lifecycle.paused: Created but not yet assigned to a campaign. No messages sent.
lifecycle.queued: Assigned to an active campaign. Picked up within 60 seconds.
lifecycle.active: First message sent. Eligible for bumps and AI replies.
lifecycle.completed: All bumps sent, booking confirmed, or lead exhausted.
lifecycle.dnd: Lead opted out (replied STOP). Never contacted again.
lifecycle.tip: To pause a specific lead manually, set their automation_status to "paused" in the lead detail panel. To pause a whole campaign, set campaign status to "Inactive".

webhooks.title: 4 - Source Adapters (CRM Webhooks)
webhooks.description: Each CRM has a dedicated intake URL. Paste the URL into your CRM's webhook settings. Leads are automatically created and queued.
webhooks.tip: Replace YOUR_KEY with the API key from <settingsLink>Settings</settingsLink>. Replace ID with the campaign ID from the campaign detail page.

instagram.title: 5 - Instagram Integration
instagram.description: Connect an Instagram Business account to sync existing DM contacts and receive inbound messages.
instagram.step1: Go to Settings → Account. Enter your Instagram User ID (your IG Business Account numeric ID) and Access Token (a long-lived Page access token from Meta).
instagram.step2: Click Sync Instagram Contacts to pull all existing DM conversation contacts from Meta's API. Each unique contact is created as a lead with status paused.
instagram.step3: Assign synced leads to a campaign to start automated outreach, or use them for manual follow-up in the Conversations inbox.
instagram.tip: Meta allows ~200 API calls per hour per token. For accounts with 200+ contacts, the sync may stop early due to rate limiting — just run it again after an hour. Duplicates are automatically skipped.

analytics.title: 6 - Analytics & Reporting
analytics.step1: Lead Scores (0–100) are recalculated every 30 minutes. They combine funnel stage (40%), engagement (30%), and activity recency (30%).
analytics.step2: Campaign Metrics (response rate, booking rate, cost per lead) are snapshotted daily at midnight. Check them under Reports.
analytics.step3: Automation Logs show every step the engine took for every lead — useful for debugging missed sends or unexpected behavior.
analytics.tip: A lead score above 70 = high intent. Prioritize manual follow-up on these.

users.title: 7 · User Management & Roles
users.description: LeadAwaker uses a role-based access model. Each user is assigned one role that determines what they can see and do.
users.step1: To invite a new user, go to Settings → Team and click Add User. Enter their name, email, and role.
users.step2: Operators can be assigned to specific accounts. They will only see leads, campaigns, and data for their assigned accounts.
users.step3: Managers and Viewers always see the subaccount view, scoped to their own account. They cannot access other accounts' data.
users.tip: Only Admins can create or delete users. Operators can view the team list but cannot change roles or remove members.

prompts.title: 8 · Prompt Library & AI Personas
prompts.description: The Prompt Library stores reusable AI persona definitions that control how the AI agent speaks, what tone it uses, and how it handles objections.
prompts.step1: Go to Prompt Library and click New Prompt. Give it a clear name (e.g., "Friendly Sales Agent" or "Dutch Appointment Setter").
prompts.step2: Write the system message. This is the instruction the AI receives before every conversation. Include: tone, language, the service being offered, how to handle objections, and when to stop pushing.
prompts.step3: Use template variables in your prompt: {{agent_name}}, {{service}}, {{company}}. These are filled automatically per-account.
prompts.step4: Link your prompt to a campaign under Campaign → Configurations. Each campaign can use a different AI persona.
prompts.tip: Best practice: keep system messages under 500 words. Be explicit about what the AI should NOT do (e.g., "Never promise discounts", "Don't mention competitors").

billing.title: 9 · Billing & Invoicing
billing.description: The Billing section tracks contracts, invoices, and expenses for each account.
billing.step1: Contracts define the agreement between you and the client — monthly fee, start date, and terms. Create them under Billing → Contracts.
billing.step2: Invoices are sent to clients for payment. You can generate invoices manually or set up recurring billing. Each invoice links to a contract.
billing.step3: Expenses (agency-only) track costs like Twilio usage, API fees, and other operational costs. Upload PDF receipts for record-keeping.
billing.tip: All amounts support BTW/VAT calculations automatically. Set your VAT rate in the account settings to have it applied to new invoices.

troubleshoot.title: 10 · Troubleshoot

1.solution: Check: campaign status = Active, lead automation_status = queued, business hours not blocking, daily limit not reached. Check Automation Logs for the lead.

2.solution: Ensure the lead's language field is set. The AI uses this to reply in the correct language.

3.solution: This means Twilio failed to send the first message. Manually reset to 'queued' in the DB and check Twilio credentials.

4.solution: Phone-based dedup is per-account. The same phone cannot be in the same account twice. Different accounts can have the same phone.

5.solution: Check Automation Logs for ai_conversation errors. Usually means OpenAI API key issue or rate limit.

6.solution: This usually means the sending number has a low trust score. Register for A2P 10DLC (US), avoid link-heavy messages, and warm up new numbers gradually (start with 50–100 messages/day).

7.solution: Review Meta's template guidelines: no promotional language in utility templates, no URL shorteners, and correct category selection. Re-submit with edits and allow 1–3 business days.

8.solution: Scores refresh every 30 minutes via a cron job. If scores are stale beyond that, check that the scoring engine is running in Automation Logs (look for 'lead_score_batch' entries).

troubleshoot.notReceivingSolution: Check: campaign status = Active, lead automation_status = queued, business hours not blocking, daily limit not reached. Check Automation Logs for the lead.
troubleshoot.wrongLanguageSolution: Ensure the lead's language field is set. The AI uses this to reply in the correct language.
troubleshoot.stuckSendingSolution: This means Twilio failed to send the first message. Manually reset to 'queued' in the DB and check Twilio credentials.
troubleshoot.duplicateLeadsSolution: Phone-based dedup is per-account. The same phone cannot be in the same account twice. Different accounts can have the same phone.
troubleshoot.aiStoppedSolution: Check Automation Logs for ai_conversation errors. Usually means OpenAI API key issue or rate limit.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Welcome to LeadAwaker! Here's a quick overview of what you'll see after logging in.
gettingStarted.step1: After logging in, you'll land on the Campaigns page. This shows all active campaigns running for your account with key metrics at a glance.
gettingStarted.step2: Use the left sidebar to navigate between pages: Campaigns, Leads, Conversations, Calendar, and Documentation (this page).
gettingStarted.step3: Click on any lead to view their full profile, conversation history, and current status in the pipeline.
gettingStarted.tip: Start by reviewing your active campaigns and their lead counts. The Leads page lets you filter by status to quickly find your hottest prospects.

what.title: What is LeadAwaker?
what.p1: LeadAwaker is an AI-powered lead follow-up system. When someone expresses interest in your service (fills a form, clicks an ad, etc.), LeadAwaker automatically reaches out via WhatsApp, nurtures the conversation, and books calls — all on autopilot.
what.p2: The AI agent replies to leads in real time, handles objections, and knows when to stop and let a human take over.

leads.title: Understanding Your Leads
leads.description: Each lead goes through a journey from first contact to booking. Here's what the status indicators mean:

stages.new: Lead just arrived, not yet contacted.
stages.contacted: First message sent. Waiting for a reply.
stages.responded: Lead replied at least once. AI is in conversation.
stages.multipleResponses: Active back-and-forth dialogue with the AI.
stages.qualified: Lead has shown interest and is being guided to book.
stages.booked: Appointment confirmed! This is the north-star outcome.
stages.closed: Deal completed — no further action needed.
stages.lost: Lead went cold or was not a fit.
stages.dnd: Lead opted out. Never contacted again.

leads.new: Lead just arrived, not yet contacted.
leads.contacted: First message sent. Waiting for a reply.
leads.responded: Lead replied at least once. AI is in conversation.
leads.qualified: Lead has shown interest and is being guided to book a call.
leads.notInterested: Lead opted out or was not a fit.

conversations.title: Reading Conversations
conversations.step1: Open the Leads page and click any lead to view their full conversation history.
conversations.step2: Messages shown on the right side are outbound (sent by the AI or your team). Messages on the left are inbound (from the lead).
conversations.step3: Look for the AI label on messages — these were generated automatically. Messages without this label were sent manually.
conversations.step4: Message status icons tell you the delivery state:
conversations.statusDeliveredDesc: confirmed arrival on the lead's device
conversations.step5: If you want to take over the conversation manually, toggle Manual Takeover on the lead detail. The AI will stop replying and you can message freely. To hand back to the AI, toggle it off again.
conversations.tip: Use Manual Takeover for high-value leads you want to handle personally. The AI won't interfere while takeover is active — but remember to turn it off when done, or the lead will stop receiving automated follow-ups.

score.title: Lead Score
score.description: Every lead has a Lead Score from 0 to 100 that estimates how likely they are to book. It's calculated from:
score.factor2: How many times they've replied and how recently (30%)
score.scoreRingsDescription: Small score rings appear on lead cards, showing at a glance how warm each lead is:
score.scoreBreakdownDescription: When you open a lead, the detail panel shows three score gauges:
score.gaugeExplanation: Lead Score is the overall composite. Engagement tracks reply frequency and recency. Activity Score measures recent pipeline movement.
score.tip: Leads with a score above 70 are your warmest prospects. Prioritize these for manual outreach.

bookings.title: Bookings
bookings.p1: When the AI detects a booking confirmation in the conversation, the lead is automatically moved to Call Booked status. The booking date and time are saved to the lead record.
bookings.p2: You can view all upcoming bookings on the Calendar page, which supports monthly, weekly, and daily views with drag-and-drop rescheduling.
bookings.tip: If a booking appears incorrect or was auto-detected by mistake, manually update the lead status from the detail panel.

accountMgmt.title: Managing Your Account
accountMgmt.description: Customize your profile and preferences from the Settings page.
accountMgmt.step1: Profile: Update your name, email, phone number, and avatar under Settings → My Profile.
accountMgmt.step2: Notifications: Choose which events trigger alerts (call booked, lead responded, AI needs takeover) and how you receive them (in-app or email). Set quiet hours to pause notifications outside business hours.
accountMgmt.step3: Timezone: Make sure your timezone is set correctly — it affects how business hours, scheduling, and calendar events are displayed.
accountMgmt.tip: Enable email notifications for "Booked" events so you never miss a new appointment, even when you're away from the dashboard.

reports.title: Reports & Insights
reports.description: Track how your campaigns are performing with key metrics available across the dashboard.
reports.responseRateDesc: Percentage of leads who replied to at least one message. Higher is better — aim for 30%+.
reports.bookingRateDesc: Percentage of leads who booked a call. This is your bottom-line conversion metric.
reports.costPerLeadDesc: Total messaging cost divided by number of leads contacted. Helps you measure ROI.
reports.avgReplyTimeDesc: Average time between outbound message and lead reply. Faster replies usually mean warmer leads.
reports.pipelineFunnelDescription: The campaign detail page shows a visual funnel of how leads move through stages:
reports.scoreDistributionDescription: Score rings appear on lead cards, showing at a glance how warm each lead is:
reports.tip: Check campaign metrics weekly to spot trends. A sudden drop in response rate may indicate message fatigue — consider refreshing your first message copy.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Can leads tell they're talking to an AI?
faq.a1: The AI is trained to sound natural and human. It doesn't announce itself as a bot unless directly asked or unless your campaign script tells it to.
faq.q2: What happens if a lead says STOP?
faq.a2: They're immediately added to the Do Not Contact list and will never be messaged again by any campaign in your account.
faq.q3: How fast does the AI reply?
faq.a3: Within seconds to a few minutes, depending on message volume. A small delay is added to simulate natural human typing.
faq.q4: Can I message a lead manually?
faq.a4: Yes — enable Manual Takeover on the lead. The AI pauses and you send messages directly from the Conversations page.
faq.q5: What if a lead doesn't respond?
faq.a5: The system sends up to 3 follow-up messages (bumps) at scheduled intervals. After all bumps are exhausted, the lead is marked Completed and automation stops.
faq.q6: How do I export my leads?
faq.a6: Open the Leads page and use the export button in the toolbar. You can export filtered results as CSV for use in spreadsheets or other tools.
faq.q7: Can I customize the AI's tone?
faq.a7: Yes — each campaign can be linked to a Prompt Library entry that defines the AI's personality, language, and conversation style. Ask your account operator to set this up.
faq.q8: What happens when I change my business hours?
faq.a8: The change takes effect immediately. Any leads currently queued for messages outside the new hours will wait until the next valid window. No messages are lost.
faq.q9: How are leads assigned to campaigns?
faq.a9: Leads are assigned when they arrive through a webhook (the campaign_id is specified in the URL) or manually through the lead detail panel.
